title: DELETE Statement
content:
### DELETE Statement

The DELETE statement removes rows from a table. Use WHERE to specify which rows to delete; omitting WHERE deletes ALL rows.

---

### DELETE Syntax

```sql
DELETE FROM table_name
WHERE condition;
```

### Basic DELETE Example

```sql
DELETE FROM employees
WHERE id = 101;

-- Delete multiple rows
DELETE FROM employees
WHERE department = 'Intern' AND hire_date < '2023-01-01';
```

### DELETE All Rows

```sql
-- Removes all rows (but slower than TRUNCATE)
DELETE FROM employees;
```

### DELETE with Subquery

```sql
DELETE FROM products
WHERE category_id IN (
    SELECT id FROM categories WHERE active = 0
);
```

### DELETE with JOIN

```sql
-- MySQL / PostgreSQL
DELETE e
FROM employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
WHERE d.name = 'Archived';

-- SQL Server
DELETE e
FROM employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
WHERE d.name = 'Archived';
```

### DELETE vs TRUNCATE vs DROP

| Operation | Speed | Can use WHERE | Rollback | Resets Identity |
|-----------|-------|---------------|----------|----------------|
| DELETE | Slowest | Yes | Yes (in transaction) | No |
| TRUNCATE | Fast | No | No (usually) | Yes |
| DROP | Fastest | N/A | No | N/A |

### Best Practice: SELECT Before DELETE

```sql
-- Preview rows before deleting
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department = 'Archived';

-- Then delete
DELETE FROM employees WHERE department = 'Archived';
```

> **Key Point:** Always use a WHERE clause unless you intend to remove all rows. Use SELECT with the same WHERE first to review what will be deleted.

quiz.question: What happens if you run DELETE FROM employees without a WHERE clause?
options: Deletes all rows in the employees table

quiz.question: Can DELETE be rolled back if used within a transaction?
options: Yes, if inside a transaction it can be rolled back
